framing: Pharmacist applying to health-tech startup requiring clinical + coding (Layer Health-style JD)

jd.title: Clinical Informatician
jd.company: Health AI Startup (Series A, MIT/Harvard founders)
jd.location: Boston, MA or New York, NY (in-person required)

requirements.text: Exceptional clinical informatician with significant experience analyzing and redesigning healthcare IT workflows
History of successfully launching new products/features in hospital settings (EPIC, Cerner, etc.)
Fluency with at least one programming language, preferably Python
Working knowledge of natural language processing (NLP) methods
Understanding of common healthcare standards and ontologies (FHIR, HL7, ICD10, CPT, RxNorm, etc.)
MD/DO who has completed primary specialty training, OR PharmD/DNP with clinical informatics fellowship or equivalent work experience
Advanced training in clinical informatics through fellowships, post-graduate training, or work experience
Willingness to work in-person in Boston or New York City

responsibilities: Perform detailed workflow analysis at client hospitals in collaboration with customer success and product teams
Contribute to an advanced interoperability layer, transforming FHIR, HL7, CCDA, and other data standards
Participate in data modeling, harmonization, normalization, and transformation efforts
Work collaboratively across cross-functional teams including engineering/research, customer success, and business
Create documentation enabling team members to self-serve on informatics topics, workflows, and data sources

expected_output.summary: Clinical pharmacist with 6 years of hospital pharmacy experience and growing clinical informatics expertise in Boston and New York. PharmD with CPOE and medication safety informatics experience at two major academic medical centers. Self-taught Python and SQL through clinical data projects. Seeking to transition fully into clinical informatics at a health-tech startup.

experience.title: Clinical Pharmacy Informaticist
bullets: Led redesign of CPOE (Computerized Physician Order Entry) medication alerts in Epic, reducing alert fatigue by 35%
Built Python scripts to analyze 2 years of medication error data (18K+ incidents) identifying top alert override patterns
Collaborated with IT team to implement FHIR-based medication reconciliation interface between Epic and pharmacy dispensing system
Authored clinical decision support rules for 12 high-risk drug interactions, reviewed by P&T committee
Trained 40+ pharmacists and residents on new Epic medication management workflows

experience.title: Clinical Pharmacist — Inpatient
bullets: Provided clinical pharmacy services on internal medicine and ICU units
Participated in pharmacy informatics committee developing medication use evaluation criteria
Used SQL queries on pharmacy data warehouse to generate monthly medication utilization reports

education: PGY-2 Pharmacy Informatics Residency — Brigham and Women's Hospital (2019-2020)

jd_requirements: products features hospital settings EPIC Cerner
FHIR HL7 ICD10 CPT RxNorm ontologies
deliberate_flaws: PharmD not MD/DO — JD prefers MD but lists PharmD as acceptable with informatics fellowship
Python is self-taught through data projects, not CS-trained
CCDA exposure only, not deep expertise
SQL skills are intermediate (pharmacy warehouse), not production database
Career changer — clinical pharmacist moving to startup, culture fit uncertain
